diagrams.description: Didactics is the science of teaching and learning, focused on the planning, implementation, and evaluation of instruction. This flowchart maps its systematic approach: the ultimate goal is to enable effective and meaningful learning. It integrates core components—instructional planning (curriculum analysis, content sequencing, learning objectives), methodological decisions (teaching methods, media, social forms), and assessment (diagnostic, formative, summative). These are informed by theoretical foundations (constructivist didactics, critical‑communicative didactics, Bildung‑centered theories) and enacted through the teaching cycle of plan‑implement‑evaluate‑reflect‑adapt. The process produces competent, critically thinking learners and meaningful educational outcomes.
